data.title: Bitcoin Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that if Bitcoin surpasses $105,000, the cumulative short liquidation intensity on major centralized exchanges (CEX) will reach $1.152 billion. Conversely, if Bitcoin falls below $102,000, the cumulative long liquidation intensity on these exchanges will amount to $1.106 billion.

BlockBeats notes that the liquidation chart does not display the exact number of contracts pending liquidation or their precise value. Instead, the chart's bars represent the relative importance of each liquidation cluster compared to nearby clusters, indicating intensity.

Therefore, the chart illustrates the potential impact on the market when the asset price reaches certain levels. A higher "liquidation bar" suggests a more intense reaction due to liquidity waves when the price hits those points.

data.title: X to Launch XMoney Payment Service in 2025, Advancing Musk’s Fintech Vision
data.description: X CEO Linda Yaccarino confirms launch timeline for XMoney as part of platform’s AI-driven ecosystem expansionX (formerly Twitter) is set to roll out its long-anticipated XMoney payment service later this year, according to CEO Linda Yaccarino. The new feature is a major component of Elon Musk’s broader vision to transform the platform into a fully integrated financial and social ecosystem.

data.title: U.S. Trade Negotiation Priorities Include Key Asian Economies
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the United States has developed a priority list for trade negotiations involving both major and minor economies. U.S. President Donald Trump's team has identified approximately 20 economies for this initiative.

The list features countries such as Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam, which are significant sources of imports for the U.S. and are targeted by Trump to reduce trade deficits. Additionally, it includes smaller partners like Fiji, Lesotho, and Mauritius.

data.title: TRUMP Team Addresses Recent Token Transfer to CEX
data.description:
According to PANews, the TRUMP team has clarified the recent transfer of 3.5 million TRUMP tokens to a centralized exchange (CEX). The official statement explained that the move aims to enhance liquidity, ensuring that both parties in transactions can access TRUMP tokens. The liquidity for this transfer was provided by the liquidity wallet established during the initial issuance. The team emphasized that all recently unlocked TRUMP tokens have been relocked and will not be moved for the next 90 days.

Earlier reports indicated that an address associated with the TRUMP team transferred 3.5 million TRUMP tokens to a CEX within the past half-hour.

data.title: BNBChain Sees Surge in Active Addresses and Transactions
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Nansen reveals a significant increase in activity on BNBChain over the past 30 days. The number of active addresses has more than doubled, rising from 1 million to over 2 million. During this period, daily transaction volumes peaked at 8 million before beginning to decline.

data.title: Market Cap of Smaller Cryptocurrencies Reaches Two-Month High
data.description: According to Odaily, the total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies excluding the top ten, referred to as 'OTHERS,' has surpassed $27.4 billion, marking a two-month high. Over the past three days, this segment has experienced a 20% increase. Additionally, the OTHERS.D index has risen by 8% in the same period, indicating a significant resurgence in interest for small and mid-cap cryptocurrency projects.

data.title: OTHERS market value exceeds 27.4 billion US dollars, up 20% in three days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market data, the total market value (OTHERS) after excluding the top ten cryptocurrencies exceeded US$27.4 billion, a two-month high, with a three-day increase of 20%. In addition, OTHERS.D rose 8% in three days, and the market heat for small and medium-sized market value projects has rebounded significantly.

data.title: Europe Implements Capital Controls Amid Digital Euro Concerns
data.description: According to PANews, CitizenX CEO Alex Recouso announced on the X platform that Europe is enforcing capital controls. Spain will impose a fine of 3,000 euros for failing to report cash withdrawals exceeding 150 euros within 24 hours. In response, Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino commented that the atmosphere surrounding the digital euro is intensifying.
